name: The Prop Center — Daily Auto-Post (3x/day)
description: Runs daily at 9am, 1pm, and 6pm. Calls the content generator, then posts to Instagram and TikTok.
how_to_import: In Make.com: Create Scenario > Import Blueprint > paste this file.

modules.name: Schedule Trigger
modules.note: Runs 3x daily. Each run generates and posts 1 of the 3 post types based on time of day.

modules.name: Call Claude API (Generate Caption)

body.system: You are the social media voice for The Prop Center, a store for balloon artists and event decorators. Be enthusiastic, peer-to-peer, never salesy.

messages.content: Write a {{post_type}} social media caption for balloon artists and event decorators. For product posts use: Available at thepropcenter.com — link in bio. Include 6-8 relevant hashtags at the end. Max 150 words.

modules.note: Replace ANTHROPIC_API_KEY with your actual key in Make.com variables.

modules.name: Get Shopify Product (for product posts)
modules.note: Connect Make's Shopify app to thepropcenter.myshopify.com. Used only on the 1pm product route.

modules.name: Post to Instagram
modules.note: For video reposts, upload the video file first via the Instagram Media Upload module.

modules.name: Post to TikTok
modules.note: TikTok requires video content. For image posts, create a slideshow video first using FFmpeg or Canva automation.

modules.name: Log to Google Sheet
modules.note: Keeps a log of everything posted so you can track what's working.

setup_checklist: 1. In Make.com, go to Connections and connect: Shopify, Instagram for Business, TikTok, Google Sheets
2. Import this blueprint as a new Scenario
3. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY as a Make variable (Variables tab in your Organization settings)
4. Create a Google Sheet called 'The Prop Center — Post Log' with columns: Date, Post Type, Caption Preview, Instagram Status, TikTok Status
5. Turn the Scenario ON and set it to Active
6. Test each route manually using the Run Once button before going live

alternative_simpler_setup.description: If Make.com is complex, use this webhook approach instead
steps: Run daily_content_generator.py via a cron job on your computer or a cheap VPS
The script outputs a JSON file with all 3 posts
Set up a Make.com webhook scenario that accepts the JSON and does the posting
This separates content generation (Python) from posting (Make.com)
